186	    def _process_z2m_device_payload(self, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
187	        """Apply device-topic JSON on the Home Assistant event loop."""
188	        action = payload.get("action")
189	
190	        # Handle lock/unlock actions with user identification (keypad PIN usage)
191	        if isinstance(action, str) and action in _Z2M_LOCK_ACTIONS:
192	            action_user = payload.get("action_user")
193	            if action_user is not None and not isinstance(action_user, bool):
194	                code_slot = parse_slot_num(action_user)
195	                if code_slot is None:
196	                    LOGGER.debug(
197	                        "Ignoring %s with non-numeric action_user %r for %s",
198	                        action,
199	                        action_user,
200	                        self.lock.entity_id,
201	                    )
202	                    return
203	                to_locked = action in _Z2M_LOCK_ACTIONS_LOCKED
204	                self.async_fire_code_slot_event(
205	                    code_slot=code_slot,
206	                    to_locked=to_locked,
207	                    action_text=action,
208	                    source_data=payload,
209	                )
210	            return
211	
212	        # Handle pin_code added / deleted (Z2M action events, not the users object)
213	        if action in ("pin_code_added", "pin_code_deleted"):
214	            action_user = payload.get("action_user")
215	            if action_user is not None:
216	                LOGGER.debug(
217	                    "Lock %s received %s for user %s",
218	                    self.lock.entity_id,
219	                    action,
220	                    action_user,
221	                )
222	                if self.coordinator:
223	                    self.hass.async_create_task(
224	                        self.coordinator.async_request_refresh()
225	                    )
226	            return
227	
228	        users_data = payload.get("users")
229	        if users_data and isinstance(users_data, dict):
230	            updates: dict[int, SlotCredential] = {}
231	            for user_id_str, user_info in users_data.items():
232	                user_id = parse_slot_num(user_id_str)
233	                if user_id is None:
234	                    LOGGER.warning(
235	                        "Skipping non-numeric Zigbee2MQTT user key %r for %s",
236	                        user_id_str,
237	                        self.lock.entity_id,
238	                    )
239	                    continue
240	
241	                if not isinstance(user_info, dict):
242	                    LOGGER.debug(
243	                        "Skipping unexpected user_info type %s for slot %s on %s",
244	                        type(user_info).__name__,
245	                        user_id_str,
246	                        self.lock.entity_id,
247	                    )
248	                    continue
249	
250	                status = user_info.get("status")
251	                pin_code_present = "pin_code" in user_info
252	                pin_raw = user_info.get("pin_code")
253	
254	                # Zigbee2MQTT often omits pin_code when expose_pin is false (default on
255	                # several Yale models). Treating that as empty makes the coordinator think
256	                # the slot is cleared, so disabling the slot skips clear_usercode while the
257	                # lock still holds the PIN. Only treat as empty when MQTT exposes the field.
258	                if status == "enabled":
259	                    if _mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value(pin_raw):
260	                        updates[user_id] = SlotCredential.known(str(pin_raw))
261	                    elif pin_code_present:
262	                        updates[user_id] = SlotCredential.empty()
263	                    else:
264	                        continue
265	                else:
266	                    updates[user_id] = SlotCredential.empty()
267	
268	            if updates and self.coordinator:
269	                LOGGER.debug(
270	                    "Lock %s received push update for slots: %s",
271	                    self.lock.entity_id,
272	                    list(updates.keys()),
273	                )
274	                self.coordinator.push_update(updates)
275	
276	        pin_code_data = payload.get("pin_code")
277	        if pin_code_data and isinstance(pin_code_data, dict):
278	            raw_user = pin_code_data.get("user")
279	            if raw_user is None:
280	                LOGGER.debug(
281	                    "Ignoring pin_code payload without user field for %s",
282	                    self.lock.entity_id,
283	                )
284	                return
285	
286	            user_id = parse_slot_num(raw_user)
287	            if user_id is None:
288	                LOGGER.warning(
289	                    "Ignoring pin_code payload with non-numeric user for %s",
290	                    self.lock.entity_id,
291	                )
292	                return
293	
294	            if user_id in self._pending_codes:
295	                future = self._pending_codes.pop(user_id)
296	                if not future.done():
297	                    user_enabled = pin_code_data.get("user_enabled", False)
298	                    pin_code = pin_code_data.get("pin_code")
299	                    if user_enabled and _mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value(pin_code):
300	                        future.set_result(str(pin_code))
301	                    else:
302	                        future.set_result(None)
303	
304	    async def _async_ensure_device_subscription(self) -> None:
305	        """Subscribe to the Z2M device topic; idempotent."""
306	        if self._push_unsubs:
307	            return
308	
309	        if not mqtt_config_entry_enabled(self.hass):
310	            raise LockDisconnected("MQTT component not available")
311	
312	        topic = self._get_topic()
313	        if not topic:
314	            raise LockDisconnected(
315	                f"Cannot subscribe for {self.lock.entity_id} — "
316	                "not a Zigbee2MQTT device or friendly name unavailable"
317	            )
318	
319	        def message_received(msg: ReceiveMessage) -> None:
320	            """Handle incoming MQTT messages (may run off the event loop)."""
321	            try:
322	                payload = json.loads(msg.payload)
323	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError) as err:
324	                LOGGER.debug(
325	                    "Ignoring invalid MQTT JSON for %s: %s",
326	                    self.lock.entity_id,
327	                    err,
328	                )
329	                return
330	
331	            self.hass.add_job(self._process_z2m_device_payload, payload)
332	
333	        try:
334	            unsub = await async_subscribe(self.hass, topic, message_received)
335	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
336	            LOGGER.error(
337	                "Failed to subscribe to MQTT for %s: %s",
338	                self.lock.entity_id,
339	                err,
340	            )
341	            raise LockDisconnected(
342	                f"Failed to subscribe to MQTT for {self.lock.entity_id}"
343	            ) from err
344	        self._register_push_unsub(unsub)
345	        LOGGER.debug("Subscribed to MQTT topic %s for %s", topic, self.lock.entity_id)
346

348	    def setup_push_subscription(self) -> None:
349	        """
350	        Subscribe via background task when still unsubscribed (e.g. reconnect).

548	    async def async_get_users(self) -> list[User]:
549	        """
550	        Read Personal Identification Number codes from all managed slots.
551	
552	        Queries Zigbee2MQTT one slot at a time over MQTT so the bridge can
553	        respond to each GET before the next. Transient publish/timeout/read
554	        failures produce an unreadable credential so the coordinator does
555	        not treat a transient MQTT error as a confirmed-empty slot and storm
556	        reprogramming after recovery.
557	        """
558	        if not mqtt_config_entry_enabled(self.hass):
559	            raise LockDisconnected("MQTT component not available")
560	
561	        if not await self.async_is_integration_connected():
562	            self._maybe_raise_wrong_bridge_disconnect()
563	            raise LockDisconnected("Lock not connected")
564	
565	        if not await self.async_is_device_available():
566	            raise LockDisconnected("Device not available")
567	
568	        get_topic = self._get_topic("get")
569	        if not get_topic:
570	            raise LockDisconnected("Could not determine MQTT topic")
571	
572	        # Get configured code slots for this lock (any LCM entry that includes this lock).
573	        code_slots = self.managed_slots
574	
575	        if not code_slots:
576	            return []
577	
578	        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
579	        slot_states: dict[int, SlotCredential] = {}
580	
581	        # Query one slot at a time so Zigbee2MQTT / firmware can answer each GET before
582	        # the next. Parallel gather + per-slot timeouts can fail the entire refresh and
583	        # leave coordinator.data empty -- sync then skips every slot (see
584	        # SlotSyncManager._resolve_slot_state).
585	        # Transient publish/timeout/read failures use the unreadable credential so sync
586	        # does not treat the slot as confirmed-empty and storm reprogramming after MQTT
587	        # recovery.
588	        for slot_num in sorted(code_slots):
589	            future = loop.create_future()
590	            self._pending_codes[slot_num] = future
591	            payload = json.dumps({"pin_code": {"user": slot_num}})
592	            try:
593	                await async_publish(self.hass, get_topic, payload)
594	            except (HomeAssistantError, OSError) as err:
595	                LOGGER.debug(
596	                    "MQTT publish failed for PIN get %s slot %s: %s",
597	                    self.lock.entity_id,
598	                    slot_num,
599	                    err,
600	                )
601	                slot_states[slot_num] = SlotCredential.unreadable()
602	                self._pending_codes.pop(slot_num, None)
603	                continue
604	
605	            try:
606	                result = await asyncio.wait_for(future, timeout=10.0)
607	            except TimeoutError:
608	                LOGGER.debug(
609	                    "Timeout waiting for PIN code response for %s slot %s",
610	                    self.lock.entity_id,
611	                    slot_num,
612	                )
613	                slot_states[slot_num] = SlotCredential.unreadable()
614	            except Exception as err:
615	                # Broad catch is intentional: the future is resolved by the MQTT
616	                # callback, and any exception from resolution (InvalidStateError,
617	                # data processing errors) should not crash the entire refresh.
618	                # CancelledError is BaseException in Python 3.11+ and propagates.
619	                LOGGER.warning(
620	                    "Unexpected error getting PIN for %s slot %s: %s",
621	                    self.lock.entity_id,
622	                    slot_num,
623	                    err,
624	                )
625	                slot_states[slot_num] = SlotCredential.unreadable()
626	            else:
627	                slot_states[slot_num] = (
628	                    SlotCredential.known(result) if result else SlotCredential.empty()
629	                )
630	            finally:
631	                self._pending_codes.pop(slot_num, None)
632	
633	        return [user_from_slot(slot, state) for slot, state in slot_states.items()]
634	
635	    async def async_hard_refresh_codes(self) -> dict[int, SlotCredential]:
636	        """Perform hard refresh and return all codes."""
637	        return await self.async_get_usercodes()

async def async_get_users(self) -> list[User]:
        """
        Read Personal Identification Number codes from all managed slots.

        Queries Zigbee2MQTT one slot at a time over MQTT so the bridge can
        respond to each GET before the next. Transient publish/timeout/read
        failures produce an unreadable credential so the coordinator does
        not treat a transient MQTT error as a confirmed-empty slot and storm
        reprogramming after recovery.
        """
        if not mqtt_config_entry_enabled(self.hass):
            raise LockDisconnected("MQTT component not available")

        if not await self.async_is_integration_connected():
            self._maybe_raise_wrong_bridge_disconnect()
            raise LockDisconnected("Lock not connected")

        if not await self.async_is_device_available():
            raise LockDisconnected("Device not available")

        get_topic = self._get_topic("get")
        if not get_topic:
            raise LockDisconnected("Could not determine MQTT topic")

        # Get configured code slots for this lock (any LCM entry that includes this lock).
        code_slots = self.managed_slots

        if not code_slots:
            return []

        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
        slot_states: dict[int, SlotCredential] = {}

        # Query one slot at a time so Zigbee2MQTT / firmware can answer each GET before
        # the next. Parallel gather + per-slot timeouts can fail the entire refresh and
        # leave coordinator.data empty -- sync then skips every slot (see
        # SlotSyncManager._resolve_slot_state).
        # Transient publish/timeout/read failures use the unreadable credential so sync
        # does not treat the slot as confirmed-empty and storm reprogramming after MQTT
        # recovery.
        for slot_num in sorted(code_slots):
            future = loop.create_future()
            self._pending_codes[slot_num] = future
            payload = json.dumps({"pin_code": {"user": slot_num}})
            try:
                await async_publish(self.hass, get_topic, payload)
            except (HomeAssistantError, OSError) as err:
                LOGGER.debug(
                    "MQTT publish failed for PIN get %s slot %s: %s",
                    self.lock.entity_id,
                    slot_num,
                    err,
                )
                slot_states[slot_num] = SlotCredential.unreadable()
                self._pending_codes.pop(slot_num, None)
                continue

            try:
                result = await asyncio.wait_for(future, timeout=10.0)
            except TimeoutError:
                LOGGER.debug(
                    "Timeout waiting for PIN code response for %s slot %s",
                    self.lock.entity_id,
                    slot_num,
                )
                slot_states[slot_num] = SlotCredential.unreadable()
            except Exception as err:
                # Broad catch is intentional: the future is resolved by the MQTT
                # callback, and any exception from resolution (InvalidStateError,
                # data processing errors) should not crash the entire refresh.
                # CancelledError is BaseException in Python 3.11+ and propagates.
                LOGGER.warning(
                    "Unexpected error getting PIN for %s slot %s: %s",
                    self.lock.entity_id,
                    slot_num,
                    err,
                )
                slot_states[slot_num] = SlotCredential.unreadable()
            else:
                slot_states[slot_num] = (
                    SlotCredential.known(result) if result else SlotCredential.empty()
                )
            finally:
                self._pending_codes.pop(slot_num, None)

        return [user_from_slot(slot, state) for slot, state in slot_states.items()]

    async def async_hard_refresh_codes(self) -> dict[int, SlotCredential]:
        """Perform hard refresh and return all codes."""
        return await self.async_get_usercodes()

file.content:
async def async_set_credential(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        credential: Credential,
        pin: str,
        *,
        name: str | None,
        source: Literal["sync", "direct"],
    ) -> WriteResult:
        """
        Set a Personal Identification Number credential on a code slot.

        Publishes a Zigbee2MQTT ``set`` payload and immediately pushes an
        optimistic coordinator update (MQTT QoS 0 gives no delivery
        guarantee; hard-refresh mitigates drift). ``user_id`` is ignored;
        slot-only providers address the credential by ``credential.slot``.
        """
        code_slot = credential.slot

        if not mqtt_config_entry_enabled(self.hass):
            raise LockDisconnected("MQTT component not available")

        if not await self.async_is_integration_connected():
            self._maybe_raise_wrong_bridge_disconnect()
            raise LockDisconnected("Lock not connected")

        set_topic = self._get_topic("set")
        if not set_topic:
            raise LockDisconnected("Could not determine MQTT topic")

        # Zigbee2MQTT set_pin_code payload format
        payload = json.dumps(
            {
                "pin_code": {
                    "user": code_slot,
                    "user_type": "unrestricted",
                    "pin_code": pin,
                    "user_enabled": True,
                }
            }
        )

        try:
            await async_publish(self.hass, set_topic, payload)
        except OSError as err:
            # Network-level publish failure (broker unreachable). Route to
            # disconnect so the reconnect path runs instead of breaking
            # per-slot.
            LOGGER.error(
                "Failed to set PIN for %s slot %s: %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                code_slot,
                err,
            )
            raise LockDisconnected(f"Failed to set PIN: {err}") from err
        except HomeAssistantError as err:
            LOGGER.error(
                "Failed to set PIN for %s slot %s: %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                code_slot,
                err,
            )
            raise LockOperationFailed(f"Failed to set PIN: {err}") from err

        LOGGER.debug(
            "Published set_pin_code for %s slot %s",
            self.lock.entity_id,
            code_slot,
        )
        # Optimistic coordinator update after publish (MQTT QoS 0); hard_refresh mitigates drift.
        self._push_credential_update(code_slot, SlotCredential.known(pin))
        return WriteResult.CONFIRMED

    async def async_delete_credential(self, ref: CredentialRef) -> bool:
        """
        Clear a Personal Identification Number from a code slot.

        Publishes a Zigbee2MQTT ``set`` payload with ``user_enabled=false``
        and ``pin_code=null`` (many locks require both to fully clear the
        slot) and immediately pushes an optimistic coordinator update.
        See ``async_set_credential`` for the OSError-versus-HomeAssistantError
        routing rationale.
        """
        code_slot = ref.slot

        if not mqtt_config_entry_enabled(self.hass):
            raise LockDisconnected("MQTT component not available")

        if not await self.async_is_integration_connected():
            self._maybe_raise_wrong_bridge_disconnect()
            raise LockDisconnected("Lock not connected")

        set_topic = self._get_topic("set")
        if not set_topic:
            raise LockDisconnected("Could not determine MQTT topic")

        # (user_enabled only is not always enough on the device).

            {

content.text:
I have the full picture now — this one is **a genuine LCM provider bug against current Zigbee2MQTT**, verified from the converter source. Here's the diagnosis:

## Root cause: the read path waits for a payload shape z2m never publishes

When `async_get_users` reads a slot, it publishes `{"pin_code": {"user": N}}` to `<topic>/get` and parks a future waiting for a **`pin_code` response object** on the device topic (`zigbee2mqtt.py:276`). But the YMF40A RL uses the Yale `lockExtend()` definition, whose only PIN-read converter is `fz.lock_pin_code_response` — and that converter publishes GetPinCode responses as a **`users` object**:

```js
const result = {users: {}};
result.users[userId] = {status: status};
if (options?.expose_pin && data.pincodevalue) {
    result.users[userId].pin_code = data.pincodevalue.toString();
}
```

There is no code path in current zigbee-herdsman-converters that emits the `{"pin_code": {user, user_enabled, pin_code}}` shape our futures wait for. The log confirms it beat-for-beat: each GET is answered ~3 seconds later by a full-state `users` publish ("received push update for slots [1, 2, 3, 4]" — z2m merges the single-user response into its cached state and republishes everything), while the pending future sits until its 10-second timeout. Four slots → the 40.039-second refresh in the log, every slot `unreadable`.

## The out_of_sync loop

The `users` payloads are processed by the push branch (`zigbee2mqtt.py:228`), which pushed *entries for all four slots* — and since sync then declared all four out_of_sync against configured PINs, those entries were `empty()`. Given the converter's status vocabulary (`0: available, 1: enabled, 3: disabled`, anything else → `not_supported_N`), that means the lock is reporting a status our branch doesn't map to occupied — everything except exactly `"enabled"` falls to `empty()`, including `not_supported_*`. Then the cycle locks in:

1. Sync writes all four PINs (visible in the log); the set path pushes optimistic `known(pin)` and returns `CONFIRMED`.
2. The lock emits `pin_code_added` actions → our handler requests a full refresh → another 40s of timeouts.
3. Meanwhile z2m keeps republishing its **cached** `users` state (it re-emits the whole cache on any attribute change), which still carries the stale pre-write entries — and our push branch takes that as fresh truth via raw `push_update`, clobbering the optimistic writes back to empty.
4. out_of_sync again → rewrite → forever. That's the "constantly polls and keeps slots out_of_sync."

## Fix plan (LCM side)

1. **Resolve the pending read futures from the `users` branch** — that *is* the GetPinCode response on current z2m. Keep the legacy `pin_code` branch for whatever shape the provider was originally built against.
2. **Fix the status projection**: `enabled` without an exposed pin → `unreadable()` (occupied, code withheld — the universal masked projection, same as Z-Wave/Matter); `available` → empty; `not_supported_*` → `unreadable()`, not empty — misreading an unknown status as confirmed-empty is exactly what triggers reprogramming storms.
3. **Route `users` pushes through `_confirm_slot`** instead of raw `push_update`, so a response that shows the slot occupied confirms the pending optimistic write instead of a stale cached republication clobbering it. The stale-cache-overwrites-fresh-write problem needs care here — z2m's `users` cache is only as fresh as the last per-user response.

One unknown remains before writing code: which exact status string the lock reports (`available`? `not_supported_N`?) and whether `expose_pin` is on — that determines branch 2's details. The reporter should capture one raw device-topic payload (z2m frontend → device → State tab, or MQTT explorer) plus their z2m version and `expose_pin` setting.

Want me to post a comment on #1335 asking for those three things (with capture instructions), and start the fix on the parts that are already confirmed?
Posting the issue comment first, then building the fix:

input.command:
gh issue comment 1335 --repo raman325/lock_code_manager --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Thanks for the detailed report and debug log — the log plus the Zigbee2MQTT converter source made the core problem clear, and a fix is in progress.

**What's happening:** when LCM reads a slot, it publishes `{"pin_code": {"user": N}}` to the `/get` topic and waits for a `pin_code` response object. But the YMF40A RL's converter answers GetPinCode through `fz.lock_pin_code_response`, which publishes the result inside the **`users`** object instead (that's the "received push update for slots [1, 2, 3, 4]" you see ~3 seconds after each get). LCM never correlates the two, so every slot read times out after 10 seconds — the 40-second refresh in your log — and the `users` payloads are then misinterpreted in a way that keeps flipping your slots to out_of_sync, which triggers the endless reprogramming you observed.

To confirm the last detail (how your lock reports slot status), could you share three things:

1. **A raw state payload from the device topic.** In the Zigbee2MQTT frontend: your lock device → **State** tab → copy the JSON (especially the `users` object). Alternatively, subscribe to `zigbee2mqtt/entrada_cerradura` with MQTT Explorer and copy one message.
2. **Your Zigbee2MQTT version.**
3. **Whether `expose_pin` is enabled** in Zigbee2MQTT (Settings → Advanced → "Expose PIN"), and if it's off, whether you're able to test with it on — without it, the lock never reveals PIN values and LCM can only verify slot occupancy, not the codes themselves.

No log needed for now — the payload JSON is the missing piece.
EOF
)"

1	"""Payload parsing tests for Zigbee2MQTT lock provider."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import asyncio
6	from types import SimpleNamespace
7	from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch
8	
9	import pytest
10

"""Payload parsing tests for Zigbee2MQTT lock provider."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from types import SimpleNamespace
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest

from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant

from custom_components.lock_code_manager.domain.models import SlotCredential
from custom_components.lock_code_manager.providers.zigbee2mqtt import (
    Zigbee2MQTTLock,
    _mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value,
)

from .conftest import _minimal_lock


def test_mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value_rejects_bool() -> None:
    """Boolean JSON must not count as a PIN payload (truthiness trap)."""
    assert _mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value(False) is False
    assert _mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value(True) is False


def test_users_enabled_without_pin_key_skips_push() -> None:
    """Do not infer EMPTY when expose_pin hides pin_code (enabled user, key absent)."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"users": {"5": {"status": "enabled"}}})
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_not_called()


def test_users_enabled_with_numeric_zero_pin_updates() -> None:
    """Numeric zero is a valid digit; it must not be treated as a missing PIN."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"users": {"2": {"status": "enabled", "pin_code": 0}}}
    )
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_called_once_with({2: SlotCredential.known("0")})


def test_users_enabled_pin_null_clears_slot() -> None:
    """Explicit null pin in MQTT means cleared at the device."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"users": {"5": {"status": "enabled", "pin_code": None}}}
    )
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_called_once_with({5: SlotCredential.empty()})


def test_users_non_numeric_slot_key_skipped() -> None:
    """Invalid user slot keys in ``users`` are ignored."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"users": {"bad": {"status": "enabled", "pin_code": "1"}}}
    )
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_not_called()


async def test_pin_code_get_disabled_or_empty_pin_sets_future_none() -> None:
    """PIN response with disabled user, empty pin, or numeric zero."""
    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    lock = _minimal_lock()

    fut_disabled = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[7] = fut_disabled
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"pin_code": {"user": 7, "user_enabled": False, "pin_code": "1234"}}
    )
    assert fut_disabled.done() and fut_disabled.result() is None

    fut_empty = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[8] = fut_empty
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"pin_code": {"user": 8, "user_enabled": True, "pin_code": ""}}
    )
    assert fut_empty.done() and fut_empty.result() is None

    fut_zero = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[9] = fut_zero
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"pin_code": {"user": 9, "user_enabled": True, "pin_code": 0}}
    )
    assert fut_zero.done() and fut_zero.result() == "0"


async def test_pin_code_deleted_schedules_refresh_task(
    hass: HomeAssistant,
    zigbee2mqtt_lock_with_device: Zigbee2MQTTLock,

lines: +    Project one Zigbee2MQTT ``users`` entry to a SlotCredential.
+    The status vocabulary comes from zigbee-herdsman-converters'
+    it are published as ``not_supported_<n>``. Mapping traps:
+    - ``enabled`` without a usable PIN value is occupied-but-withheld
+      (``expose_pin`` off hides the code entirely), so it projects to
+      unreadable -- treating it as empty would make sync reprogram a slot
+      that already holds the right code.
+    - The one exception: an explicit ``pin_code: null`` on an enabled user
+      means the broker exposes the field and the device reports no code,
+      not empty, for the same reprogramming-storm reason.

users_data = payload.get("users")
        if users_data and isinstance(users_data, dict):
            states: dict[int, SlotCredential] = {}
            for user_id_str, user_info in users_data.items():
                user_id = parse_slot_num(user_id_str)
                if user_id is None:
                    LOGGER.warning(
                        "Skipping non-numeric Zigbee2MQTT user key %r for %s",
                        user_id_str,
                        self.lock.entity_id,
                    )
                    continue

                if not isinstance(user_info, dict):
                    LOGGER.debug(
                        "Skipping unexpected user_info type %s for slot %s on %s",
                        type(user_info).__name__,
                        user_id_str,
                        self.lock.entity_id,
                    )
                    continue

                states[user_id] = _project_z2m_user_state(user_info)

            # The converter answers GetPinCode through the users object
            # (fz.lock_pin_code_response), not through a pin_code response
            # payload -- resolve the pending read here or every slot read
            # times out (issue #1335). At most one read is pending at a
            # time (async_get_users queries slots sequentially), so cached
            # entries for other slots cannot satisfy a future they don't
            # belong to.
            for user_id, state in states.items():
                if (future := self._pending_codes.pop(user_id, None)) is not None:
                    if not future.done():
                        future.set_result(state)

            # Zigbee2MQTT republishes its full cached state on every
            # attribute change, so most users payloads restate old entries
            # rather than report changes. Applying them verbatim lets a
            # stale cache entry overwrite the optimistic push from a write
            # that the device already accepted -- the slot flips back to
            # its pre-write state and sync reprograms it forever (issue
            # #1335). Gate on the previous payload so only entries that
            # actually changed reach the coordinator.
            changed = {
                user_id: state
                for user_id, state in states.items()
                if self._last_users_states.get(user_id) != state
            }
            self._last_users_states.update(states)
            if changed and self.coordinator:
                LOGGER.debug(
                    "Lock %s received push update for slots: %s",
                    self.lock.entity_id,
                    list(changed),
                )
                for user_id, state in changed.items():
                    self._confirm_slot(user_id, state)

content.text: `_minimal_lock` builds a real dataclass instance, so `_confirm_slot` machinery works. Updating the behavior-changed tests:

input.old_string:
def test_users_enabled_without_pin_key_skips_push() -> None:
    """Do not infer EMPTY when expose_pin hides pin_code (enabled user, key absent)."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"users": {"5": {"status": "enabled"}}})
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_not_called()
input.new_string:
def test_users_enabled_without_pin_key_pushes_unreadable() -> None:
    """An enabled user with expose_pin hiding pin_code is occupied-but-withheld."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"users": {"5": {"status": "enabled"}}})
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_called_once_with(
        {5: SlotCredential.unreadable()}
    )


def test_users_not_supported_status_pushes_unreadable() -> None:
    """An unrecognized status must not be misread as confirmed-empty."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"users": {"3": {"status": "not_supported_5"}}})
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_called_once_with(
        {3: SlotCredential.unreadable()}
    )


def test_users_republication_of_unchanged_state_not_repushed() -> None:
    """Zigbee2MQTT republishes its full cached state; unchanged entries stay quiet.

    Without the delta gate, a stale cached entry republished after LCM's
    optimistic write flips the slot back and sync reprograms it forever
    (issue #1335).
    """
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    payload = {"users": {"2": {"status": "enabled", "pin_code": "1234"}}}

    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(payload)
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_called_once_with(
        {2: SlotCredential.known("1234")}
    )

    lock.coordinator.push_update.reset_mock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(payload)
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_not_called()

    # A genuine change still gets through.
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"users": {"2": {"status": "available"}}})
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_called_once_with({2: SlotCredential.empty()})


async def test_users_payload_resolves_pending_read() -> None:
    """GetPinCode responses arrive via the users object (issue #1335).

    The converter (fz.lock_pin_code_response) publishes the response inside
    ``users``; the pending read future must resolve from it instead of
    timing out.
    """
    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()

    fut_known = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[2] = fut_known
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"users": {"2": {"status": "enabled", "pin_code": "1234"}}}
    )
    assert fut_known.done() and fut_known.result() == SlotCredential.known("1234")
    assert 2 not in lock._pending_codes

    fut_hidden = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[3] = fut_hidden
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"users": {"3": {"status": "enabled"}}})
    assert fut_hidden.done() and fut_hidden.result() == SlotCredential.unreadable()

    fut_available = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[4] = fut_available
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"users": {"4": {"status": "available"}}})
    assert fut_available.done() and fut_available.result() == SlotCredential.empty()

async def test_users_payload_resolves_pending_read() -> None:
    """GetPinCode responses arrive via the users object (issue #1335).

    The converter (fz.lock_pin_code_response) publishes the response inside
    ``users``; the pending read future must resolve from it instead of
    timing out.
    """
    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()

    fut_known = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[2] = fut_known
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"users": {"2": {"status": "enabled", "pin_code": "1234"}}}
    )
    assert fut_known.done() and fut_known.result() == SlotCredential.known("1234")
    assert 2 not in lock._pending_codes

    fut_hidden = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[3] = fut_hidden
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"users": {"3": {"status": "enabled"}}})
    assert fut_hidden.done() and fut_hidden.result() == SlotCredential.unreadable()

    fut_available = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[4] = fut_available
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"users": {"4": {"status": "available"}}})
    assert fut_available.done() and fut_available.result() == SlotCredential.empty()

"""Payload parsing tests for Zigbee2MQTT lock provider."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from types import SimpleNamespace
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

import pytest

from homeassistant.core import HomeAssistant

from custom_components.lock_code_manager.domain.models import SlotCredential
from custom_components.lock_code_manager.providers.zigbee2mqtt import (
    Zigbee2MQTTLock,
    _mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value,
)

from .conftest import _minimal_lock


def test_mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value_rejects_bool() -> None:
    """Boolean JSON must not count as a PIN payload (truthiness trap)."""
    assert _mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value(False) is False
    assert _mqtt_payload_pin_has_code_value(True) is False


def test_users_enabled_without_pin_key_skips_push() -> None:
    """Do not infer EMPTY when expose_pin hides pin_code (enabled user, key absent)."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"users": {"5": {"status": "enabled"}}})
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_not_called()


def test_users_enabled_with_numeric_zero_pin_updates() -> None:
    """Numeric zero is a valid digit; it must not be treated as a missing PIN."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"users": {"2": {"status": "enabled", "pin_code": 0}}}
    )
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_called_once_with({2: SlotCredential.known("0")})


def test_users_enabled_pin_null_clears_slot() -> None:
    """Explicit null pin in MQTT means cleared at the device."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"users": {"5": {"status": "enabled", "pin_code": None}}}
    )
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_called_once_with({5: SlotCredential.empty()})


def test_users_non_numeric_slot_key_skipped() -> None:
    """Invalid user slot keys in ``users`` are ignored."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"users": {"bad": {"status": "enabled", "pin_code": "1"}}}
    )
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_not_called()


async def test_pin_code_get_disabled_or_empty_pin_sets_future_none() -> None:
    """PIN response with disabled user, empty pin, or numeric zero."""
    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    lock = _minimal_lock()

    fut_disabled = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[7] = fut_disabled
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"pin_code": {"user": 7, "user_enabled": False, "pin_code": "1234"}}
    )
    assert fut_disabled.done() and fut_disabled.result() is None

    fut_empty = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[8] = fut_empty
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"pin_code": {"user": 8, "user_enabled": True, "pin_code": ""}}
    )
    assert fut_empty.done() and fut_empty.result() is None

    fut_zero = loop.create_future()
    lock._pending_codes[9] = fut_zero
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"pin_code": {"user": 9, "user_enabled": True, "pin_code": 0}}
    )
    assert fut_zero.done() and fut_zero.result() == "0"


async def test_pin_code_deleted_schedules_refresh_task(
    hass: HomeAssistant,
    zigbee2mqtt_lock_with_device: Zigbee2MQTTLock,
) -> None:
    """Z2M action payloads request a coordinator refresh via async task."""
    lock = zigbee2mqtt_lock_with_device
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock.coordinator.async_request_refresh = AsyncMock(return_value=None)

    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": "pin_code_deleted", "action_user": 4})
    await hass.async_block_till_done()

    lock.coordinator.async_request_refresh.assert_awaited_once()


def test_pin_code_get_invalid_user_type_returns_early() -> None:
    """Non-numeric ``pin_code.user`` does not touch pending futures."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    fut = MagicMock(spec=["cancel", "done", "set_result"])
    fut.done.return_value = False
    lock._pending_codes[1] = fut  # type: ignore[assignment]
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(
        {"pin_code": {"user": "notint", "user_enabled": True, "pin_code": "1"}}
    )
    fut.done.assert_not_called()
    fut.set_result.assert_not_called()


async def test_mqtt_payload_invalid_json_ignored(
    hass: HomeAssistant,
    zigbee2mqtt_lock_with_device: Zigbee2MQTTLock,
) -> None:
    """Subscription callback skips invalid JSON payloads."""
    lock = zigbee2mqtt_lock_with_device
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    with (
        patch(
            "custom_components.lock_code_manager.providers.zigbee2mqtt.mqtt_config_entry_enabled",
            return_value=True,
        ),
        patch(
            "custom_components.lock_code_manager.providers.zigbee2mqtt.async_subscribe",
            new_callable=AsyncMock,
        ) as mock_subscribe,
    ):
        mock_subscribe.return_value = lambda: None
        lock.setup_push_subscription()
        await hass.async_block_till_done()
        cb = mock_subscribe.call_args[0][2]
        cb(SimpleNamespace(payload=b"not json {{{"))
        await hass.async_block_till_done()

    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_not_called()


def test_keypad_unlock_action_fires_code_slot_event() -> None:
    """keypad_unlock with action_user fires async_fire_code_slot_event."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event = MagicMock()

    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": "keypad_unlock", "action_user": 3})

    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event.assert_called_once_with(
        code_slot=3,
        to_locked=False,
        action_text="keypad_unlock",
        source_data={"action": "keypad_unlock", "action_user": 3},
    )


def test_keypad_lock_action_fires_code_slot_event() -> None:
    """keypad_lock with action_user fires event with to_locked=True."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event = MagicMock()

    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": "keypad_lock", "action_user": 1})

    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event.assert_called_once_with(
        code_slot=1,
        to_locked=True,
        action_text="keypad_lock",
        source_data={"action": "keypad_lock", "action_user": 1},
    )


def test_lock_action_without_action_user_does_not_fire_event() -> None:
    """Lock action without action_user is ignored (manual key turn, no PIN)."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event = MagicMock()

    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": "manual_lock"})

    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event.assert_not_called()


def test_lock_action_with_non_numeric_user_is_ignored() -> None:
    """Lock action with non-numeric action_user is ignored."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event = MagicMock()

    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": "keypad_unlock", "action_user": "bad"})

    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event.assert_not_called()


def test_rf_unlock_action_fires_event() -> None:
    """rf_unlock with action_user fires event."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event = MagicMock()

    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": "rf_unlock", "action_user": 5})

    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event.assert_called_once_with(
        code_slot=5,
        to_locked=False,
        action_text="rf_unlock",
        source_data={"action": "rf_unlock", "action_user": 5},
    )


def test_unknown_action_does_not_fire_event() -> None:
    """An unrecognized action value is ignored entirely."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event = MagicMock()

    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": "something_else", "action_user": 1})

    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event.assert_not_called()


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("action", "expected_locked"),
    [
        ("lock", True),
        ("unlock", False),
        ("manual_lock", True),
        ("manual_unlock", False),
        ("rf_lock", True),
    ],
)
def test_all_lock_actions_fire_event_with_correct_to_locked(
    action: str, expected_locked: bool
) -> None:
    """All supported lock/unlock actions fire events with correct to_locked."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event = MagicMock()

    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": action, "action_user": 2})

    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event.assert_called_once()
    call_kwargs = lock.async_fire_code_slot_event.call_args[1]
    assert call_kwargs["to_locked"] is expected_locked
    assert call_kwargs["code_slot"] == 2
    assert call_kwargs["action_text"] == action


def test_non_string_action_is_ignored() -> None:
    """Non-string action values (e.g., list, dict) do not raise TypeError."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event = MagicMock()

    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": ["lock"], "action_user": 1})
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": 123, "action_user": 1})

    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event.assert_not_called()


def test_boolean_action_user_is_ignored() -> None:
    """Boolean action_user (True/False) is not treated as a valid slot ID."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event = MagicMock()

    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": "keypad_unlock", "action_user": True})
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload({"action": "keypad_lock", "action_user": False})

    lock.async_fire_code_slot_event.assert_not_called()

git add custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zigbee2mqtt.py tests/providers/zigbee2mqtt/test_payload.py && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(zigbee2mqtt): read PIN responses from the users payload; gate cached republications

Zigbee2MQTT answers GetPinCode through the users object
(fz.lock_pin_code_response), not a pin_code response payload, so every
slot read timed out. Resolve pending reads from users entries, project
enabled-but-withheld and unrecognized statuses to unreadable instead of
empty, and only push entries that changed since the previous payload --
Zigbee2MQTT republishes its full cached state on every attribute
change, and applying stale entries verbatim reprograms slots forever
(#1335).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_015tvvgVhy5F12XVyv6RQBv7
EOF
)" && git push -u origin fix/z2m-users-payload-reads && gh pr create --title "fix(zigbee2mqtt): read PIN responses from the users payload; gate cached republications" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
# Proposed change

Fixes the read-timeout / out_of_sync reprogramming loop reported in #1335 (Yale YMF40A RL). Verified against the zigbee-herdsman-converters source: the Yale `lockExtend()` definition answers GetPinCode via `fz.lock_pin_code_response`, which publishes the result **inside the `users` object** — there is no code path in current Zigbee2MQTT that emits the `{"pin_code": {user, user_enabled, pin_code}}` response shape the provider's pending-read futures were waiting on. Three coupled fixes:

1. **Resolve pending slot reads from `users` payloads.** Each GET is answered by a full-state publish containing the freshly merged entry for the queried user; the pending future now resolves from it instead of burning a guaranteed 10-second timeout per slot (the 40-second refreshes in the issue's log). At most one read is pending at a time (`async_get_users` queries sequentially), so cached entries for other slots can't satisfy a future they don't belong to. The legacy `pin_code` response branch is kept.

2. **Fix the status projection** (shared by reads and pushes, per the `lockUserStatus` vocabulary `available`/`enabled`/`disabled`):
   - `enabled` without a usable PIN value → `unreadable()` (occupied-but-withheld — `expose_pin` off hides the code). Previously the push path skipped these entirely, and the read path had no way to represent them. Treating occupancy-with-hidden-code as anything but unreadable makes sync reprogram slots that already hold the right code — the same universal masked projection the Z-Wave and Matter providers use.
   - explicit `pin_code: null` on an enabled user still projects to `empty()` (the broker exposes the field and reports no code — existing behavior).
   - unrecognized statuses (`not_supported_*`) → `unreadable()`, not `empty()`.

3. **Delta-gate `users` pushes.** Zigbee2MQTT republishes its entire cached state on every attribute change, so most `users` payloads restate stale entries rather than report changes. Applying them verbatim let a stale cache entry overwrite the optimistic push from a write the device had already accepted — flipping the slot back to its pre-write state and reprogramming it forever (the loop in #1335). Entries now reach the coordinator (via `_confirm_slot`, matching the other push providers) only when they differ from the previous payload; fresh GetPinCode responses always differ when state actually changed, and the read path stays authoritative regardless of the gate.

One aspect intentionally awaits the reporter's raw payload (requested on the issue): which exact status string their lock reports. The projection above is safe for every value in the converter's vocabulary plus its `not_supported_*` fallback, so the fix does not depend on the answer.

## Type of change

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [x] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [ ] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information

- This PR fixes or closes issue: fixes #1335

## Checklist

content.text:
Both deliverables for #1335 are done:

**Issue comment posted** asking the reporter for the three missing data points: a raw device-topic state payload (with capture instructions for the z2m frontend or MQTT Explorer), their Zigbee2MQTT version, and their `expose_pin` setting.

**PR #1336 open**: https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/pull/1336 — full suite green (1258 passed), `prek` clean. Three coupled fixes:

1. **Pending slot reads resolve from `users` payloads** — the shape z2m actually publishes GetPinCode responses in (verified in `fz.lock_pin_code_response`). This kills the guaranteed 10s-per-slot timeouts. The sequential-read design turned out to be load-bearing here: only one future is ever pending, so a full-cached-state publish can't satisfy the wrong slot's read.
2. **Corrected status projection**: `enabled` with a hidden PIN → `unreadable` (was skipped in pushes, unrepresentable in reads), unknown statuses → `unreadable` instead of `empty` — misreading unknown as confirmed-empty is precisely what fuels reprogramming storms.
3. **Delta gate on `users` pushes**: z2m republishes its whole cached state on any attribute change, and applying those stale restatements verbatim is what kept clobbering the optimistic writes back to empty — the engine of the infinite out_of_sync loop. Only changed entries now reach the coordinator, routed through `_confirm_slot` like the other push providers.

The one open thread: the reporter's raw payload will tell us which status string their lock actually reports. The projection is safe for the converter's whole vocabulary including its `not_supported_*` fallback, so the fix doesn't hinge on the answer — but it's worth confirming before release, and their payload may also tell us whether they need `expose_pin` on to get value-level (rather than occupancy-level) sync.

gh issue comment 1335 --repo raman325/lock_code_manager --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Perfect, that's exactly what was needed — and your setup is the well-behaved case: plain `enabled` statuses and `expose_pin` on with readable PIN values.

Your payload confirms the full picture. Two things were compounding:

1. Every slot read waited for a response format your converter never publishes, so each read burned a 10-second timeout (the 40-second refreshes in your log).
2. Zigbee2MQTT republishes its full cached state on every attribute change, and its cached `pin_code` values go stale the moment LCM writes a new code (nothing re-reads the lock after a set). Each republication pushed the pre-write PINs back over LCM's view → out_of_sync → rewrite → repeat.

Both are fixed in #1336: reads now resolve from the `users` object your converter actually publishes (~3 seconds per slot, real values), and stale cached republications no longer override fresh writes. With `expose_pin` on, LCM will verify your actual PIN values, not just slot occupancy.

The fix will be in the next release — I'll note here when it's out so you can update and confirm.
EOF
)"

content.text:
Reply posted. Summary of where #1335 stands:

- **Reporter's data confirms the diagnosis and validates PR #1336 as-is** — no changes needed to the fix. Their z2m 2.12.1 payload shows plain `enabled` statuses with `expose_pin` on, so post-fix they get the best-case behavior: ~3-second value-level reads and genuine PIN verification.
- The payload also retroactively explains the loop precisely: it was the stale-cached-republication clobber (fix part 3), not the status-projection issue (fix part 2) — though part 2 remains correct hardening for `expose_pin`-off users, who are the z2m default.
- I've told them the fix lands in the next release and that we'll ping the issue when it's tagged.

PR #1336 is ready for your review/merge — once it's in a release, the loose ends on this issue are just the reporter's confirmation.

content.content: === INLINE by Copilot @ custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zigbee2mqtt.py:316: The delta-gate updates `_last_users_states` even when `self.coordinator` is not attached yet. In the BaseLock lifecycle, provider `async_setup()` runs before the coordinator is created, and MQTT retained messages can arrive during that window. If that happens, `_last_users_states` will be populated but no push will occur, and subsequent identical republishes will be gated out after the coordinator attaches—leaving the coordinator with no initial push-derived state until a real change/poll succeeds. Consider only applying the delta-gate (and updating `_last_users_states`) when a coordinator is present. === REVIEW by copilot-pull-request-reviewer [COMMENTED]: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes a Zigbee2MQTT provider sync loop where PIN reads were timing out (causing repeated out_of_sync reprogramming) by resolving pending reads from the `users` payload, improving status-to-credential projection (including unreadable/withheld PIN cases), and gating cached-state republications so stale cached entries don’t overwrite optimistic writes. **Changes:** - Resolve pending GET PIN reads from Zigbee2MQTT `users` payload entries (while keeping legacy `pin_code` response handling). - Project `users` status/PIN combinations into `SlotCredential` with explicit handling for “enabled but PIN withheld” and unknown statuses. - Delta-gate `users` republishes so only changed slot states are confirmed/pushed, preventing stale cache churn. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zigbee2mqtt.py | Updates payload handling to resolve pending reads from `users`, improve state projection, and gate/confirm push updates to avoid stale-cache loops. | | tests/providers/zigbee2mqtt/test_payload.py | Adds/updates tests covering unreadable projections, delta-gating behavior, and pending-read resolution via `users`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

# Zigbee2MQTT republishes its full cached state on every
            # attribute change, so most users payloads restate old entries
            # rather than report changes. Applying them verbatim lets a
            # stale cache entry overwrite the optimistic push from a write
            # that the device already accepted -- the slot flips back to
            # its pre-write state and sync reprograms it forever (issue
            # #1335). Gate on the previous payload so only entries that
            # actually changed reach the coordinator.
            changed = {
                user_id: state
                for user_id, state in states.items()
                if self._last_users_states.get(user_id) != state
            }
            self._last_users_states.update(states)
            if changed and self.coordinator:
                LOGGER.debug(
                    "Lock %s received push update for slots: %s",
                    self.lock.entity_id,
                    list(changed),
                )
                for user_id, state in changed.items():
                    self._confirm_slot(user_id, state)

                }

                if changed:
                    LOGGER.debug(

                        self.lock.entity_id,
                        list(changed),
                    )
                    for user_id, state in changed.items():
                        self._confirm_slot(user_id, state)

def test_users_payload_before_coordinator_does_not_poison_delta_gate() -> None:
    """A retained payload arriving before coordinator attach must not gate out
    the first post-attach republication (it should still seed initial state)."""
    lock = _minimal_lock()
    payload = {"users": {"2": {"status": "enabled", "pin_code": "1234"}}}

    assert lock.coordinator is None
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(payload)

    lock.coordinator = MagicMock()
    lock._process_z2m_device_payload(payload)
    lock.coordinator.push_update.assert_called_once_with(
        {2: SlotCredential.known("1234")}
    )
EOF

input.command: gh api repos/raman325/lock_code_manager/pulls/1336/comments/3561383957/replies -f body="Fixed in 7db29b4 — confirmed real: \`async_setup\` subscribes before the coordinator exists (deliberately, so the first poll doesn't race the subscription), and retained messages deliver on subscribe. The gate now only records payloads once a coordinator is attached, so a pre-attach snapshot can't suppress the first post-attach republication. Worth noting the blast radius was small either way — the coordinator's initial state comes from the read path (\`async_get_usercodes\`), which this PR makes actually work — but the pre-attach snapshot was still wrong to keep. Regression test added (\`test_users_payload_before_coordinator_does_not_poison_delta_gate\`)." 2>&1 | grep -o '"html_url":"[^"]*discussion[^"]*"' | head -1

input.content:
---
name: feedback-scrub-pins-from-payloads
description: Zigbee2MQTT state payloads contain cleartext PINs when expose_pin is on — never quote user payloads publicly without masking
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

When users share Zigbee2MQTT device-state payloads (or any lock diagnostics)
on public issues, the `users` object contains **cleartext PIN codes** whenever
`expose_pin` is enabled. In issue #1335 Raman had to manually edit the
reporter's comment to mask their real codes.

**Why:** these are live physical-access credentials on a public tracker.

**How to apply:** never quote PIN values from user-provided payloads in
comments, PRs, or logs — reference slots by number and describe values
("readable", "masked") instead. When asking users for payloads, tell them to
replace PIN values before posting. When writing LCM debug logging, keep the
existing practice of hashing PINs (`pin#<hash>`).

When users share Zigbee2MQTT device-state payloads (or any lock diagnostics)
on public issues, the `users` object contains **cleartext PIN codes** whenever
`expose_pin` is enabled. In issue #1335 Raman had to manually edit the
reporter's comment to mask their real codes.

**Why:** these are live physical-access credentials on a public tracker.

**How to apply:** never quote PIN values from user-provided payloads in
comments, PRs, or logs — reference slots by number and describe values
("readable", "masked") instead. When asking users for payloads, tell them to
replace PIN values before posting. When writing LCM debug logging, keep the
existing practice of hashing PINs (`pin#<hash>`).

# Memory Index

- [project_matter_data_model.md](project_matter_data_model.md) — Future direction to formalize lock/user/PIN data model using Matter DoorLock cluster as reference architecture
- [project_credential_mgmt_refactor.md](project_credential_mgmt_refactor.md) — Approved design: migrate all providers onto unified User→Credential model (zwave_js accessControl + matter), seam at provider boundary, 1:1:1 UX
- [feedback_no_acronyms.md](feedback_no_acronyms.md) — Spell out acronyms in code comments instead of using abbreviations
- [feedback_comment_deviations_not_defaults.md](feedback_comment_deviations_not_defaults.md) — Comment the deviation/trap, not the justification for using the native/expected mechanism
- [feedback_idiomatic_python.md](feedback_idiomatic_python.md) — Prefer comprehensions over loops, next() over for/break, concise idiomatic expressions
- [feedback_pr_template.md](feedback_pr_template.md) — Always use .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md when creating PRs
- [feedback_test_setup.md](feedback_test_setup.md) — Tests must use proper setup/teardown, not manual hass.data manipulation
- [project_matter_test_seam_migration.md](project_matter_test_seam_migration.md) — Follow-up to PR #1276: migrate 86 matter tests off the _get_matter_client/_get_matter_node patch seam to the integration harness, then remove wrappers + consolidate resolution
- [reference_wiki_repo.md](reference_wiki_repo.md) — Wiki repo path: /Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager.wiki/
- [project_test_refactor_plan.md](project_test_refactor_plan.md) — Provider test refactor: Matter done, zwave_js next, then other providers, then integration tests
- [feedback_caplog_checks.md](feedback_caplog_checks.md) — Minimize caplog text assertions, prefer checking parameters or structured data
- [feedback_tests_with_changes.md](feedback_tests_with_changes.md) — Tests go with code changes in same PR; test-only PRs only for refactoring existing tests
- [feedback_no_nonlocal.md](feedback_no_nonlocal.md) — Never use nonlocal/local/global; use mutable containers or restructure instead
- [project_resilience_refactor.md](project_resilience_refactor.md) — Incremental plan to consolidate retry/backoff/sync logic and fix layer violations
- [feedback_worktrees_subagents.md](feedback_worktrees_subagents.md) — Use worktrees and subagents for parallel isolated work; keep PRs focused, drive side tasks through subagents
- [feedback_no_inline_imports.md](feedback_no_inline_imports.md) — Never use inline/local imports; restructure modules to avoid circular dependencies
- [project_v2_release.md](project_v2_release.md) — Lock Code Manager 2.0.0 released 2026-04-03 with SlotCode, new providers, blueprints
- [feedback_pr_breaking_change.md](feedback_pr_breaking_change.md) — Only include Breaking Change section when there is an actual breaking change (auto tagger keys on section presence)
- [feedback_no_args_returns_docstrings.md](feedback_no_args_returns_docstrings.md) — Don't add Google-style Args:/Returns: sections to docstrings; prefer prose explaining intent
- [feedback_no_amend_after_pr.md](feedback_no_amend_after_pr.md) — Never amend commits after PR creation; commit forward, squash-on-merge handles cleanup
- [project_tick_consolidation.md](project_tick_consolidation.md) — Future refactor to move more sync logic into the tick to reduce race conditions
- [project_matter_live_testing.md](project_matter_live_testing.md) — Matter lock live testing status and remaining items
- [project_fullstack_provider_tests.md](project_fullstack_provider_tests.md) — Plan to add E2E tests running providers through full LCM setup lifecycle
- [feedback_status_inline_strip.md](feedback_status_inline_strip.md) — Single-line status info renders as a thin inline strip between sections, not wrapped in lcm-section chrome
- [feedback_specs_local_only.md](feedback_specs_local_only.md) — Design specs live locally only (docs/ is gitignored); write them but never commit
- [feedback_run_full_test_suite.md](feedback_run_full_test_suite.md) — Run the full suite (pytest tests/) before pushing, not a curated subset; CI caught a test a subset missed
- [project_password_credential_expansion.md](project_password_credential_expansion.md) — Roadmap for password (and other) credential type support; Option A wired in PR #1227, Option B (entity-type scoping) deferred until UX is decided
- [project_credential_base_hoisting_pattern.md](project_credential_base_hoisting_pattern.md) — PR #1227's final architecture: capability-derived decisions in BaseLock, providers are pure writes; inventory of every hoist
- [feedback_base_helper_verb_consistency.md](feedback_base_helper_verb_consistency.md) — Base orchestration helpers use the same verbs as the primitives they wrap: _set_credential / _delete_credential, not _put / _drop
- [project_zwave_js_uc_delete_value_db_asymmetry.md](project_zwave_js_uc_delete_value_db_asymmetry.md) — Upstream node-zwave-js bug: accessControl.deleteCredential UC path doesn't normalize paired userCode value DB the way legacy property-set path does; causes zwave-js-ui to hide cleared slots on UC locks
- [project_zwave_credential_cc_abstraction.md](project_zwave_credential_cc_abstraction.md) — node-zwave-js v15.23.4+ unifies User Code CC and User Credential CC under accessControl API; PR #1227 does NOT drop 500-series support
- [project_pr1227_sync_state_design.md](project_pr1227_sync_state_design.md) — Sync "in sync" predicate includes name + every managed credential type, smart granular writes, slot=user_id invariant preserved
- [feedback_trace_through_layers.md](feedback_trace_through_layers.md) — When reasoning about Z-Wave behavior, trace through driver+server+client+helper before drawing conclusions; pull latest in each local checkout first
- [project_user_tag_idempotency.md](project_user_tag_idempotency.md) — Approved: LCM identifies its own lock users by `lcm:{slot}:{display}` tag (canonical colon-delimited form). Always create LCM's own users; drop slot=user_id (U3C) and credential_index=slot (Matter) invariants; decouple user lifecycle from credential lifecycle.
- [feedback_back_compat_user_facing_only.md](feedback_back_compat_user_facing_only.md) — Only user-facing surfaces carry back-compat burden. Internal helpers/signatures evolve freely in major releases. The three user-facing back-compat concerns: tag format migration, dropping number_of_uses, calendar→entity key.
- [feedback_fail_loud_on_unencodable_identifier.md](feedback_fail_loud_on_unencodable_identifier.md) — When a system uses an external identifier to recover its state, refuse to write at all if the identifier can't be encoded. Includes the "best-effort fields evolve with the contract" corollary.
- [reference_ha_prod_deploy.md](reference_ha_prod_deploy.md) — Raman's HA prod: SSH alias `ha`, deploy path `/config/custom_components/lock_code_manager/` (old `lcm/` folder was invalid/deleted), restart via `docker restart homeassistant`, logs via `docker logs homeassistant`
- [reference_ha_text_entity_render_validation.md](reference_ha_text_entity_render_validation.md) — HA TextEntity.state raises ValueError at render time if value is outside native_min/max; capability-derived bounds must widen to admit the current value
- [feedback_ha_unload_vs_remove_entry.md](feedback_ha_unload_vs_remove_entry.md) — HA's `async_unload_entry` runs in 4 scenarios; persistent state cleanup belongs in `async_remove_entry` (runs only on actual entry deletion)
- [feedback_one_bug_can_hide_another.md](feedback_one_bug_can_hide_another.md) — Fixing a bug that suppresses state often exposes previously-invisible bugs; expect a follow-on issue and budget for it
- [project_matter_sdk_exception_hierarchy.md](project_matter_sdk_exception_hierarchy.md) — `matter_server.common.errors.MatterError` is INDEPENDENT of `HomeAssistantError`; always include both in matter-side except clauses
- [feedback_no_unsourced_claims.md](feedback_no_unsourced_claims.md) — Don't fabricate specific external-product details (firmware versions, vendor behavior) to make hypotheses sound concrete; cite or omit
- [project_zwave_uc_u3c_dispatch_bug.md](project_zwave_uc_u3c_dispatch_bug.md) — node-zwave-js AccessControl FIXME: U3C dispatch wins whenever advertised, even when surface is empty — leading hypothesis for issue #1251
- [Zwave UC zero-slots root cause (#1251)](project_zwave_uc_zero_slots_root_cause.md) — caps=0 is driver-side; PR #1252 bypass cannot fix writes (driver asserts same predicate)
- [feedback_compare_versions_for_regressions.md](feedback_compare_versions_for_regressions.md) — When an old-version comparison runs against an open regression, audit preserved-but-identical logic for symptom-fit, not just "did it carry over"
- [project_provider_availability_entity_state.md](project_provider_availability_entity_state.md) — Availability gates should defer to entity HA availability, not re-derive integration internals (Matter Bug 1 of #1268, PR #1271)
- [project_phase2_lifecycle_gotchas.md](project_phase2_lifecycle_gotchas.md) — PR #1259 lifecycle traps: CONFIRMED must clear stale unverified flag; don't expire+sync same tick; UC fallback confirms inline so can't use seam pending bookkeeping
- [Log levels: expected conditions are info, not warning](feedback_log_levels_expected_conditions.md) — warn only when user action may be needed; states already surface unavailability
- [UC bridge: report shim + reconcile read](project_uc_report_shim.md) — zwave_js bridges 3 upstream UC defects for add-on users on driver 15.25.0; self-sunsets; remove once min driver includes zwave-js#8930 (v16 branch)

content.content:
=== raman325 @ 2026-07-10T20:00:40Z:
Perfect, that's exactly what was needed — and your setup is the well-behaved case: plain `enabled` statuses and `expose_pin` on with readable PIN values.

Your payload confirms the full picture. Two things were compounding:

1. Every slot read waited for a response format your converter never publishes, so each read burned a 10-second timeout (the 40-second refreshes in your log).
2. Zigbee2MQTT republishes its full cached state on every attribute change, and its cached `pin_code` values go stale the moment LCM writes a new code (nothing re-reads the lock after a set). Each republication pushed the pre-write PINs back over LCM's view → out_of_sync → rewrite → repeat.

Both are fixed in #1336: reads now resolve from the `users` object your converter actually publishes (~3 seconds per slot, real values), and stale cached republications no longer override fresh writes. With `expose_pin` on, LCM will verify your actual PIN values, not just slot occupancy.

The fix will be in the next release — I'll note here when it's out so you can update and confirm.
=== PR 1336 latest:
Copilot @ custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zigbee2mqtt.py:358:
This `pin_code` branch assumes the earlier `user` parsing is safe, but `parse_slot_num()` will coerce JSON booleans (`True`/`False`) to `1`/`0` because it uses `int(value)`. That could incorrectly resolve a pending future for slot 0/1. Add an explicit `isinstance(raw_user, bool)` guard before calling `parse_slot_num()` (mirroring the `action_user` handling).

def parse_slot_num(value: object) -> int | None:
    """
    Convert a slot identifier to an int, or return None if not convertible.

    Mirrors ``int(value)`` while collapsing the ``TypeError``/``ValueError``
    that providers otherwise catch when a lock reports a non-numeric slot key.
    Call sites remain responsible for their own logging and skip/return flow.
    """
    try:
        return int(value)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
    except TypeError, ValueError:
        return None

input.old_string:
def parse_slot_num(value: object) -> int | None:
    """
    Convert a slot identifier to an int, or return None if not convertible.

    Mirrors ``int(value)`` while collapsing the ``TypeError``/``ValueError``
    that providers otherwise catch when a lock reports a non-numeric slot key.
    Call sites remain responsible for their own logging and skip/return flow.
    """
    try:
        return int(value)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
    except TypeError, ValueError:
        return None
input.new_string:
def parse_slot_num(value: object) -> int | None:
    """
    Convert a slot identifier to an int, or return None if not convertible.

    Mirrors ``int(value)`` while collapsing the ``TypeError``/``ValueError``
    that providers otherwise catch when a lock reports a non-numeric slot key.
    JSON booleans are rejected rather than coerced (``int(True)`` is 1, so a
    malformed ``true`` would otherwise silently address slot 1).
    Call sites remain responsible for their own logging and skip/return flow.
    """
    if isinstance(value, bool):
        return None
    try:
        return int(value)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
    except TypeError, ValueError:
        return None

def parse_slot_num(value: object) -> int | None:
    """
    Convert a slot identifier to an int, or return None if not convertible.

    Mirrors ``int(value)`` while collapsing the ``TypeError``/``ValueError``
    that providers otherwise catch when a lock reports a non-numeric slot key.
    JSON booleans are rejected rather than coerced (``int(True)`` is 1, so a
    malformed ``true`` would otherwise silently address slot 1).
    Call sites remain responsible for their own logging and skip/return flow.
    """
    if isinstance(value, bool):
        return None
    try:
        return int(value)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
    except TypeError, ValueError:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""
Shared helpers for provider implementations.

Provider-internal utilities that don't belong in the integration-wide
``util.py``. Currently: slot-tagging used by providers whose lock APIs
identify codes by user-name rather than by slot number (Schlage, Akuvox,
and -- once the unified-user migration lands -- Matter and Z-Wave User
Credential CC).

Three formats are emitted today; a fourth (legacy) is still tolerated on
read for locks whose names were written by older releases.

* Canonical ``lcm:<slot>:<name>`` -- the consolidated format every new
  write uses when the lock's ``max_user_name_length`` can fit the full
  ``lcm:<slot>:`` prefix. Two colons delimit the slot field clearly for
  visual parseability on the lock UI.
* Compact ``lcm<slot>`` -- the charset-constrained fallback, used when
  the lock's firmware rejects the colons (Matter spec is permissive but
  many firmwares restrict ``userName`` to alphanumeric). Drops the
  display portion but keeps the LCM ownership marker, so the lock-side
  name is unambiguous about who owns the user record. Strictly better
  than the slot-only fallback for coexistence with other controllers,
  which is why providers try this tier first before falling all the way
  back to ``str(slot)``.
* Slot-only ``<digits>`` -- the deepest fallback, used when neither
  canonical nor compact fit. Just the slot number, written as
  ``str(slot)``. Ambiguous with any external user named with only
  digits, but only encountered on locks with absurdly small name limits
  or firmwares that reject even ``lcm<slot>``, so the tradeoff is
  accepted.
* Legacy ``[LCM:<slot>] <name>`` -- read-only. Older Schlage/Akuvox
  releases wrote this format; ``parse_tag`` still recognizes it so the
  integration can identify its own users on locks that haven't been
  rewritten yet. The next write naturally replaces the lock-stored name
  with the canonical format.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import re

_LEGACY_SLOT_TAG_RE = re.compile(r"^\[LCM:(\d+)\]\s*(.*)")
_TAG_RE = re.compile(r"^lcm:(\d+):\s*(.*)")
_COMPACT_TAG_RE = re.compile(r"^lcm(\d+)$")
_SLOT_ONLY_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d+)$")


def make_tagged_name(slot_num: int, name: str | None = None) -> str:
    """Return a code name in the canonical ``lcm:<slot>:`` format."""
    base = name or f"Code Slot {slot_num}"
    return f"lcm:{slot_num}:{base}"


def make_compact_tagged_name(slot_num: int) -> str:
    """
    Return a code name in the compact ``lcm<slot>`` format.

    Charset-safe (alphanumeric only) fallback used when a lock's
    firmware rejects the colons in the canonical format. Drops the
    display portion but preserves the LCM ownership marker so the
    on-lock name doesn't collide with externally-created users in the
    way that the bare slot-only fallback would.
    """
    return f"lcm{slot_num}"


def parse_tag(name: str) -> tuple[int | None, str]:
    """
    Parse a Lock Code Manager slot tag, tolerant of all known formats.

    Returns ``(slot_num, friendly_name)`` when any format matches, or
    ``(None, original_name)`` otherwise. Match priority: canonical,
    legacy, compact, slot-only. The compact and slot-only branches are
    charset-/length-constrained fallbacks emitted by Matter (and
    eventually other providers) when the lock rejects the canonical
    name; their display portion is empty because the name only carries
    the slot binding. The legacy ``[LCM:<slot>]`` format is read-only
    -- nothing emits it anymore -- but is still recognized so older
    lock-stored names continue to identify as LCM-owned until the next
    write rewrites them in the canonical format. Bare digits being
    treated as a slot tag is intentional but ambiguous with external
    users whose names happen to be digit-only; the ambiguity is the
    cost of preserving the slot binding on constrained locks.
    """
    if match := _TAG_RE.match(name):
        return int(match.group(1)), match.group(2)
    if match := _LEGACY_SLOT_TAG_RE.match(name):
        return int(match.group(1)), match.group(2)
    if match := _COMPACT_TAG_RE.match(name):
        return int(match.group(1)), ""
    if match := _SLOT_ONLY_RE.match(name):
        return int(match.group(1)), ""
    return None, name


def parse_slot_num(value: object) -> int | None:
    """
    Convert a slot identifier to an int, or return None if not convertible.

    Mirrors ``int(value)`` while collapsing the ``TypeError``/``ValueError``
    that providers otherwise catch when a lock reports a non-numeric slot key.
    Call sites remain responsible for their own logging and skip/return flow.
    """
    try:
        return int(value)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
    except TypeError, ValueError:
        return None
